user_outcome: A SwornAgent operator can configure a generic webhook and an optional mobile push destination, receive stable safe event notifications with links to the board, and see bounded delivery outcomes in the durable outbox.
rationale: This delivers the requested webhook and mobile paging outcome in #109 after the best-effort notification defects confirmed by #108. It consumes C-05 from S07, owns the C-09 generic webhook, C-10 outbound edge, and C-11 mobile-push wire contracts, and uses the envelope's optional canonical board URL. Providers are transport adapters only: they serialize a stable event projection, report typed attempt outcomes to the outbox, and may link to the board, but they never embed or execute a mutation. The generic webhook remains provider-neutral; the mobile adapter uses an ntfy-compatible HTTP contract without adding an SDK.
in_scope: Define versioned generic-webhook-v1 and mobile-push-v1 projections from C-05 using stable notification/event IDs, attention class, safe title/body, source revision, and optional canonical C-06 board URL.

Preserve `sworn account set-webhook <url>` as the generic webhook configuration gesture while moving delivery through the S07 outbox; add explicit mobile destination configuration and secret-safe status output.

Store destination secrets in the shared credential envelope, store only opaque destination references in the operations database, and migrate an existing webhook_url without deleting provider or account fields.

Implement the generic webhook with JSON content type, stable idempotency and event headers, an optional HMAC signature over exact body bytes, no redirects, bounded connect/response/total deadlines, and response-body-free diagnostics.

Implement an ntfy-compatible mobile POST using standard-library HTTP with safe topic URL validation, title/priority/tags headers, and a single view action whose URL opens the read board for the event target.
Forbid notification-carried mutation URLs, command bodies, bearer credentials, local file paths, and provider-specific operation semantics; all remote actions must be initiated through S11.
Map HTTP and transport results into S07's closed delivered/retryable/permanent classification, honoring a valid Retry-After only when it remains inside outbox policy bounds.
Provide a no-secret `sworn notifications test <destination>` reachability gesture that enqueues and delivers a synthetic safe test envelope through the real dispatcher.
out_of_scope: Outbox schema, leasing, retry scheduling, replay, and attention taxonomy are owned by S07-notification-outbox.
Email, SMS, Slack, Telegram, inbound provider callbacks, and hosted notification fan-out are outside #109.
Remote command authentication and mutation endpoints are owned by S11; this slice emits view links only.
The web read API and board implementation are owned by S09 and S10; this slice only validates and carries their canonical URL.
The twelve planned slices in 2026-07-11-contract-edge-gates remain excluded, including their loop max-turn, provider, dry-run, design-authority, and guard-fidelity work.

effort_complexity.rationale: The wire formats and HTTP safety controls are well understood, but configuration migration, two adapters, outbox integration, signing, security fixtures, and command reachability make the implementation broad.

risks.description: A mobile action button becomes an alternate unauthenticated command surface.
risks.mitigation: Allow only a browser view action to a validated C-06 URL; reject mutation verbs, command parameters, and provider callbacks in both config and serialized headers.

risks.description: Destination URLs or signing secrets leak through the database, notification output, redirects, or HTTP errors.
risks.mitigation: Keep secrets in the credential envelope, persist references only, disable redirects, never log response bodies, and inject recognizable canaries into transport tests and public-safe scans.

risks.description: Retry behavior differs by adapter and bypasses the durable policy.
risks.mitigation: Both adapters return the same closed DeliveryResult to S07; neither sleeps or retries internally. Contract tests run identical status/timeout cases against both.

risks.description: Credential-envelope migration overwrites provider keys, login state, or an existing destination.
risks.mitigation: Use field-preserving credential updates and a command-level sequence test spanning provider setup, login, both destinations, refresh, and logout.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN a generic-webhook-v1 attempt is delivered THE SYSTEM SHALL POST one C-09 JSON body whose notification_id and operation_event_id equal the C-05 envelope and whose event header is stable across retries (C-03, C-05, C-09, N-03).
WHEN a webhook signing secret is configured THE SYSTEM SHALL attach the C-09 HMAC signature computed over the exact transmitted body under C-10 credential isolation (C-05, C-09, C-10, N-03).

WHEN a mobile-push-v1 attempt is delivered THE SYSTEM SHALL emit one C-11 ntfy-compatible view action whose URL equals the optional canonical board URL in C-05 and contains no command or mutation parameter (C-05, C-11, N-03).

IF a destination redirects, exceeds its deadline, returns 408/425/429/5xx, or returns another non-2xx status THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL report the C-10 permanent or retryable DeliveryResult classification to the outbox without reading a response body into diagnostics (C-05, C-10, N-03).
IF a destination URL violates C-10 or a mobile topic violates C-11 THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL reject configuration before storing credentials or enqueueing a test notification (C-05, C-10, C-11, N-03).

WHEN existing provider keys and account fields surround webhook/mobile configuration, login refresh, and logout THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve every field not owned by the invoked command and SHALL retain both destination settings after logout (C-05, N-03).

IF a notification fixture includes canary credentials, model payloads, response bodies, absolute worktree paths, or mutation instructions THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL transmit, persist, and print none of those values through C-09 or C-11 under C-10 (C-03, C-05, C-09, C-10, C-11, N-03).

WHEN the built binary runs `sworn notifications test <destination>` against a real local HTTP fixture THE SYSTEM SHALL enqueue through S07, deliver through C-09 or C-11, print the durable notification ID and delivered state, and exit zero (C-05, C-09, C-10, C-11, N-03).
